title: cuphea inaequalifolia
intro: Cuphea inaequalifolia, also known as the yellow cuphea or Mexican heather, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small, yellow or white flowers and narrow, lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Its medicinal properties make it beneficial for various health conditions, including fever reduction, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Cuphea inaequalifolia can be used as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some traditional remedies for its potential to soothe sore throats and ease respiratory congestion.
